messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following legal services firm client data notice for GDPR compliance:

"LexPartners collects your name, contact details, identification documents, financial information, and case-related communications to provide legal services. Processing is necessary for the performance of our contract with you and to comply with anti-money laundering regulations. Files are retained for 7 years after matter closure per professional regulations. You have the right to access and correct your data by contacting your case manager. Our DPO is at dpo@lexpartners.com. We will notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach. We use a cloud document management system (iManage, EU-hosted) under a signed DPA."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) for AML compliance both stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Legal case files frequently contain health, financial distress, criminal history, or relationship data. No acknowledgement of special category data risk is included.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Legal service delivery and AML compliance are clearly separated purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for legal matter management.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 7-year post-matter retention stated and grounded in professional regulatory requirements.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access and rectification referenced. Rights to erasure (limited by legal hold obligations), portability, restriction, and objection not stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] iManage named as processor with EU hosting and signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU-hosted iManage confirms EEA processing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@lexpartners.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (special category data in legal files not addressed), Art. 15-21 (portability, restriction, objection rights absent)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that legal case files may contain special category data and state safeguards
- Add portability, restriction, and objection rights — and where applicable, explain legal professional privilege limitations on erasure
- Consider noting the right to complain to the ICO (Art. 77)
